class.name: ResonanceRuntimeConfig
class.brief_description: Runtime configuration resource for Nexus Resonance. Structure aligned with Steam Audio Settings.
class.description: Nexus Resonance runtime configuration resource. Create or link in ResonanceRuntime node (Add Child Node > ResonanceRuntime). Structure and group order follow Steam Audio Settings: HRTF Settings, Ray Tracer Settings, Occlusion Settings, Real-time Reflections, Baked Reverb, Baked Pathing, Simulation Update, Reflection Effect, Hybrid Reverb, Reverb Output, Physics. Emits reflection_type_changed, pathing_enabled_changed, and audio_frame_size_changed when those settings change. Godot routing: [member bus] and [member reverb_bus_name]; the reverb effect bus sends to the same target as [method get_bus_effective].

signal.name: audio_frame_size_changed
signal.description: Emitted when audio_frame_size changes (inspector value: 0 = Auto, or 256 / 512 / 1024 / 2048). In the editor, [ResonanceRuntime] reconnects the native audio engine when the server is already initialized (brief audio interruption).

method.name: get_effective_realtime_rays
method.description: Returns [param realtime_rays] unchanged on all platforms. [param os_name] is reserved for future per-OS caps; pass [method OS.get_name] when calling.

method.name: get_bus_effective
method.description: Returns effective bus for Direct + Pathing (player output). Empty config = Master.

method.name: get_reverb_bus_name_effective
method.description: Returns effective reverb bus name. Empty config = ResonanceReverb.

method.name: get_hrtf_sofa_effective
method.description: Returns the active custom SOFA for engine init: entry from [member hrtf_sofa_assets] at [member hrtf_sofa_selected_index], or the first list entry with SOFA data if that slot is empty. Returns null if the list is empty or no entry has valid data (default embedded HRTF).

method.name: get_config
method.description: Returns config dictionary for [method ResonanceServer.init_audio_engine]. Includes sample_rate from AudioServer. Does not include [member bus] or [member reverb_bus_name]; Godot bus routing is handled by [ResonanceRuntime] only.

method.name: create_default
method.description: Creates a default runtime config for editor/fallback when no ResonanceRuntime in scene.

members.member: Sample rate override. 0 = Use Godot Mix Rate (follow project settings). Other values = override; mismatch may cause audio issues (no resampling).

Steam Audio processing block size (samples per channel per effect apply). Should match Godot’s mix buffer for stable reverb routing. Auto (0) = closest of 256 / 512 / 1024 / 2048 from Project Settings [code]audio/driver/output_latency[/code] and mix rate. Manual = override for latency vs CPU; wrong values risk dropouts. After changing while the game runs from the editor, [ResonanceRuntime] triggers [method ResonanceServer.reinit_audio_engine] via [signal audio_frame_size_changed].
Ambisonic order for reverb encoding: 1st Order, 2nd Order, or 3rd Order. Higher = more spatial detail, more CPU.

Upper bound for simulator / ReflectionMixer IR allocation (seconds): caps [code]simulation_settings.maxDuration[/code] and bundled convolution IR size. Not the per-run simulation length (see [member realtime_simulation_duration]).

[code]IPLSimulationSharedInputs.numRays[/code] for realtime reflection simulation. [code]0[/code] = Off (no realtime rays). Presets include 8, 16, 32, 64, … up to 8192; higher counts cost more CPU. Uses [member scene_type]; without Embree/OpenCL features the native layer may use the built-in (Default) tracer.
Realtime reflection bounces (1–64).

Ray tracer backend: Default (0) = built-in Phonon; Embree (1) = Intel CPU; Radeon Rays (2) = OpenCL (Windows 64-bit oriented); Custom (3) = [code]IPL_SCENETYPE_CUSTOM[/code] using Godot 3D physics [code]intersect_ray[/code] ([member physics_ray_collision_mask]). Custom runs Steam Audio simulation on the main thread; [ResonanceGeometry] meshes are not uploaded. Bakes and mesh assets still use the built-in tracer. Avoid [code]physics/3d/run_on_separate_thread[/code] if possible (see Godot physics threading notes).
Physics ray collision mask when [member scene_type] is Custom. [code]-1[/code] means all layers. Same semantics as [code]PhysicsRayQueryParameters3D.collision_mask[/code].

When [member scene_type] is Custom: [code]IPLSimulationSettings.rayBatchSize[/code] and batched Godot trace callbacks when [code]> 1[/code] (Phonon BatchedReflectionSimulator). Clamped to 1–256 in the native layer. Ignored for Default/Embree/Radeon (Nexus passes batch size 1).
OpenCL device type when scene_type is Radeon Rays or reflection_type is TrueAudio Next: GPU (0), CPU (1), or Any (2). Helps when GPU has OpenCL issues.
OpenCL device index (0 = first matching device). Use when multiple GPUs present.
Min distance for irradiance sampling (0.05–10.0 m). Only when Realtime Rays > 0.

Impulse response length (seconds) for real-time simulation shared inputs ([code]IPLSimulationSharedInputs.duration[/code]) each run. Distinct from [member max_reverb_duration] (allocator / mixer IR cap). Range 0.1–10.0 s.
Diffuse samples per reflection point (8–64).

Applies only to [ResonancePlayer] sources with reflections type Use Global. Other players can override per source. [code]0[/code] = Baked (probe reverbs): indirect energy is stored per probe and is not the same as per-source ray occlusion through walls. [code]1[/code] = Realtime (set [member realtime_rays] > 0): ray-traced reflections follow the acoustic scene (Embree/Default/Radeon) or Godot physics (Custom), so sealed rooms and geometry affect indirect paths like Steam Audio realtime. Each source uses one path per update.
Reverb algorithm: Convolution (0), Parametric (1), Hybrid (2), TrueAudio Next (3, AMD GPU only).
Hybrid reverb: length (seconds) of IR used for convolution before parametric tail. Only when reflection_type is Hybrid.
Hybrid reverb: overlap percent (0–100) for crossfade between convolution and parametric parts.
When [ResonancePlayerConfig] uses global direct binaural, apply HRTF on the dry / direct path (vs speaker panning). Requires a loaded HRTF.
Apply HRTF when decoding Ambisonic convolution / mixer output to stereo (reflection / reverb wet path).
Apply HRTF in the pathing effect (indirect paths). When [ResonancePlayerConfig] pathing HRTF uses global, this value is the default.
Use virtual surround instead of HRTF.

Direct path without HRTF: [code]IPLSpeakerLayout[/code] channel count (1 Mono, 2 Stereo, 4 Quad, 6 5.1, 8 7.1). With surround and Ambisonics encode, non-HRTF sources use [code]iplAmbisonicsPanningEffect[/code]; otherwise [code]iplPanningEffect[/code]. Godot [code]AudioStreamPlayback[/code] output remains stereo (fold-down inside the player). Invalid values become stereo at runtime. Requires [method ResonanceServer.reinit_audio_engine] after change while running.
HRTF gain in dB for the embedded default HRTF. With custom SOFA, added (in dB) to each asset's volume before gain conversion.
Normalization for embedded default HRTF only: None (0) or RMS (1). Custom SOFA uses [member ResonanceSOFAAsset.norm_type] on the asset.
SOFA HRTF resources. [member hrtf_sofa_selected_index] selects the active file. Empty = default embedded HRTF.
Index into [member hrtf_sofa_assets] for the active SOFA (clamped at runtime).
Use bilinear HRTF interpolation.
Baked reverb influence radius in meters.

Extra wet attenuation by distance (meters). [code]0[/code] = off (no extra scale on reflections/pathing wet beyond simulation). When > [code]0[/code], wet gain is full at or below this distance and fades linearly to [code]0[/code] by [code]2 ×[/code] this distance (convolution/TAN mixer feed, parametric/hybrid wet, pathing stereo). Does not replace per-source [member ResonancePlayerConfig.max_distance] for the direct path.
Target bus for Direct + Pathing (player output). Empty = Master. Fallback when players use Global bus override.

Bus that hosts [ResonanceAudioEffect] for convolution / TAN wet and the reverb activator. Empty = ResonanceReverb. Godot send for this bus follows [method get_bus_effective] (same as Direct + Pathing runtime bus).

How much transmission damps reverb (0–1). 0 = no damping, 1 = full damping (reverb scaled by material transmission). Only consulted for baked REVERB paths when the wet-path occlusion damping is on (see [member apply_occlusion_to_baked_reflections] and [member ResonancePlayerConfig.apply_occlusion_to_baked_reflections_override]). Realtime reflections and STATICSOURCE/STATICLISTENER already encode source→listener occlusion in the IR. A per-source value can be set via [member ResonancePlayerConfig.reverb_transmission_amount_input].

When on, baked REVERB reflections also scale their wet input by the direct-path occlusion/transmission factor. Default is off: direct-line occlusion cannot distinguish "source around a corner in the same open room" (listener room still excited via openings/diffraction; realtime convolution stays loud) from "source truly sealed away". Turning this on dampens both cases uniformly, making baked REVERB sound less plausible than realtime for the common "around the corner" case. Prefer per-source [code]reflections_type = Realtime[/code] or the STATICSOURCE bake workflow for accurate outdoor-to-indoor reflections. Can be overridden per source via [member ResonancePlayerConfig.apply_occlusion_to_baked_reflections_override] (e.g. enable it just for outdoor thunder / rain sources while indoor sources keep plausible wet feeds). Has no effect on realtime, STATICSOURCE or STATICLISTENER paths.

When on (default), the reflection-effect input gain is multiplied by the per-source 3D distance attenuation factor so baked/realtime reverb fades with distance. Matches Steam Audio Unity's Spatialize pipeline, where the AudioSource rolloff is baked into the inBuffer before [code]iplReflectionEffectApply[/code]. Turn this off only for projects that want constant-gain wet feeds (e.g. 2D ambience beds or custom distance handling). Per-source override via [member ResonancePlayerConfig.apply_distance_curve_to_reflections_override] beats this global flag.

Reflections sampling mode (where baked REVERB chooses its probe from). 0=Listener-centric (probe nearest the listener; recommended), 1=Source-centric (probe nearest the source; legacy behavior). Note: currently this only affects baked REVERB probe lookup. Steam Audio realtime reflections trace rays from the listener in the core API, so this setting does not yet change realtime ray origin. Can be overridden per source via [member ResonancePlayerConfig.reflections_sampling_mode_override].
Enable pathing (multi-path sound propagation). Requires baked pathing in Probe Volumes.
Pathing: normalize EQ on path effect output. Prevents pathing from sounding too bright.
Runtime pathing: Steam Audio numVisSamples (1–16). Lower = less CPU; bake quality uses bake_pathing_num_samples on ResonanceBakeConfig.
Default path validation when a ResonancePlayerConfig uses Use Global (-1) for [member ResonancePlayerConfig.path_validation_override]. Validates baked paths against dynamic geometry each update.

Default find-alternate-paths when a player uses Use Global (-1) for [member ResonancePlayerConfig.find_alternate_paths_override]. Only applies when path validation is effectively enabled for that source.

Direct-effect transmission frequency mode (Steam Audio [code]IPLTransmissionType[/code]): FreqIndependent (0) = one blended coefficient; FreqDependent (1) = low/mid/high bands. Controls how material transmission is applied in the direct processor, not geometry-edge blending. Steam Audio [code]IPLSimulationInputs[/code] has no separate “volumetric transmission” switch (only [code]numTransmissionRays[/code] for path depth).

Global default for Steam Audio [code]numTransmissionRays[/code] (max surfaces along the transmission path, 1–256): initial simulator source state and when [member ResonancePlayerConfig.max_transmission_surfaces_override] is **Use Global** ([code]0[/code]), or as C++ fallback when reading per-source [member ResonancePlayerConfig.max_transmission_surfaces].
Occlusion: Raycast (0) = binary hit test; Volumetric (1) = fractional occlusion from samples ([code]numOcclusionSamples[/code]). Independent of [member transmission_type].
Simulator cap for volumetric occlusion ([code]maxNumOcclusionSamples[/code]). Per-source occlusion samples are clamped to this value.
Maximum simultaneous sources for realtime reflection simulation ([code]maxNumSources[/code]) and TrueAudio Next [code]maxSources[/code].

Minimum seconds between worker applications of queued dynamic instanced-mesh transforms to Steam Audio (one [code]iplSceneCommit[/code] batch per apply). [code]0[/code] = apply every simulation tick that has queued updates. [code]> 0[/code] can lower [code]rtcCommitScene[/code] cost on large Embree scenes at the cost of stale occlusion during motion. Pair with [method ResonanceGeometry.flush_dynamic_acoustic_transform] when motion stops.

[b]Reflections Sim Interval[/b] — Minimum seconds between scheduling reflection-heavy simulation ([code]iplSimulatorRunReflections[/code]). [b]0[/b]: schedule every worker tick (highest CPU). [b]0.1[/b]: at most one reflection scheduling wave every ~100 ms (good default). Higher values reduce CPU; realtime IR updates may lag slightly. Independent of [member pathing_sim_interval] and [member direct_sim_interval].

[b]Pathing Sim Interval[/b] — Minimum seconds between scheduling pathing-heavy simulation ([code]iplSimulatorRunPathing[/code]). Same semantics as [member reflections_sim_interval]; increase to stagger expensive pathing from reflections (e.g. path validation / alternate paths).

[b]Direct Sim Interval[/b] — Minimum seconds between [code]iplSimulatorRunDirect[/code] runs on worker ticks that do not already schedule reflections or pathing heavy passes (or when those are skipped). Use this to throttle **direct-path occlusion/transmission/air absorption** updates independently of [member reflections_sim_interval] and [member pathing_sim_interval]. [b]0[/b] (default): run direct simulation on every worker wake where the worker runs (same responsiveness as before this knob existed). Small values (e.g. [b]0.02[/b]–[b]0.05[/b]): fewer direct updates when reflections/pathing are already sparse — lowers CPU; occlusion may react slightly slower until the next eligible tick. When a tick schedules reflections or pathing, direct simulation still follows the worker’s combined scheduling for that wake.
Fraction of CPU cores for Steam Audio simulation threads (0–1). Default 0.15; higher = more parallelism for reflections/pathing, more CPU.
Enable perspective correction for reverb.
Perspective correction factor (0.5–2.0).
